calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing Re: Writ Of Administrative Mandamus
Off Calendar Nov-19-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER WADE ROBERTSON'S Notice Of Hearing Re: Writ Of Administrative Mandamus.
APPEARANCES: Marc J. Zilversmit, Esq. 431-3472 for petitioner & moving party; David A. Carrillo, Deputy Atty General 510/622-2215 for respondent & opposing party.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling - THE WRIT IS DENIED. OFFICER RYAN HAD A REASONABLE BASIS TO EFFECT THE STOP. THE ADMINISTRATIVE LAW JUDGE'S ANALYSIS IS CONSISTENT WITH THE EVIDENCE AND CONSISTENT WITH THE RESPONSIBILITY OF THE TRIER OF FACT TO REVIEW AND WEIGH THE EVIDENCE. THERE WAS NO BASIS FOR PETITIONER TO REFUSE TO TAKE A DUI TEST. ANY DELAY ASSOCIATED WITH THE PRODUCTION IS NOT A BASIS FOR OVERTURNING THE DECISION. Court sustained respondent's objection re extra record evidence. Order signed in open court. In addition, the Court granted petitioner's request for a further stay of enforcement for 30 days. (302/PJM/JW) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

Notice And Motion For Peremptory Writ Of Mandate

Appearances:

Marc Zilversmit (431.3472) for petitioner and moving party

Argued. Respondent moved to supplement the record and provide the petitioner with digital copies of the roll. The Court ruled as follows: respondents request to supplement the record is granted, petitioner's oral request for fees is denied. Parties are to meet and confer regarding a briefing schedule and the matter may then be re-noticed. (302/PJM/AY) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
